verses.text: The psalmist cries day and night to 'the God of my salvation,' his soul full of troubles and drawing near the grave. Even in deepest gloom he keeps praying. Faith may be in darkness, yet still it clings to God as its salvation.

The psalmist cries day and night to 'the God of my salvation,' his soul full of troubles and drawing near the grave. Even in deepest gloom he keeps praying. Faith may be in darkness, yet still it clings to God as its salvation.

The psalmist cries day and night to 'the God of my salvation,' his soul full of troubles and drawing near the grave. Even in deepest gloom he keeps praying. Faith may be in darkness, yet still it clings to God as its salvation.
He feels counted with the dead, laid in the lowest pit, God's wrath heavy upon him like waves. His anguish is overwhelming. There are depths where the believer feels crushed under God's hand.
He feels counted with the dead, laid in the lowest pit, God's wrath heavy upon him like waves. His anguish is overwhelming. There are depths where the believer feels crushed under God's hand.
He feels counted with the dead, laid in the lowest pit, God's wrath heavy upon him like waves. His anguish is overwhelming. There are depths where the believer feels crushed under God's hand.
He feels counted with the dead, laid in the lowest pit, God's wrath heavy upon him like waves. His anguish is overwhelming. There are depths where the believer feels crushed under God's hand.
His friends are taken from him, and he is shut up, his eye wasting with grief, yet still he stretches out his hands to God. Isolation deepens his pain. When all human comfort fails, prayer remains.
His friends are taken from him, and he is shut up, his eye wasting with grief, yet still he stretches out his hands to God. Isolation deepens his pain. When all human comfort fails, prayer remains.
He asks whether God will show wonders to the dead, or His mercy be declared in the grave. He pleads life as the time to praise. The sufferer reasons that he can serve God better alive than dead.
He asks whether God will show wonders to the dead, or His mercy be declared in the grave. He pleads life as the time to praise. The sufferer reasons that he can serve God better alive than dead.
He asks whether God will show wonders to the dead, or His mercy be declared in the grave. He pleads life as the time to praise. The sufferer reasons that he can serve God better alive than dead.
Each morning his prayer comes before God, yet he asks, 'LORD, why castest thou off my soul?' He persists though unanswered. Faith can keep crying even when heaven seems silent.
Each morning his prayer comes before God, yet he asks, 'LORD, why castest thou off my soul?' He persists though unanswered. Faith can keep crying even when heaven seems silent.

Afflicted from youth and near death, he feels God's terrors closing in, with darkness his only companion. The psalm ends without resolution. Even this dark cry, kept toward God, is a kind of faith, teaching that the believer may walk through deep shadow and still hold on.
